summary: Large item-system resplit of the monolithic src/melee/it/it_2725.c into several topic-oriented translation units. The PR carved out ithitbox, itmaplib, itmaterial, iteffect, itanimlist, it_279C, and itzako, updated GALE01 splits/symbols and configure.py, added matching headers, and redirected includes/users across item, fighter, and ground code. The author explicitly says the split was guided by repeated floats/strings plus filename evidence from asserts/objects/strings; all new units were matching and linked except itmaplib, which remained NonMatching in configure.py. Bot metrics reported +4396 matched code bytes, +34912 linked code bytes, +17004 matched data bytes, and 157 new matches, with many apparent broken matches representing code moved out of it_2725 into the new units.
what_changed: Reduced it_2725.c from a broad address range, 0x80272560-0x8027CF00, to 0x80272560-0x802753DC, leaving only the still-unclear core portion in that TU.

Added new split entries in config/GALE01/splits.txt for ithitbox.c, itmaplib.c, itmaterial.c, iteffect.c, itanimlist.c, it_279C.c, and itzako.c, with text/data/sdata/sdata2 ranges matching the original it_2725 address space.
Updated configure.py so it_2725.c, ithitbox.c, itmaterial.c, iteffect.c, itanimlist.c, it_279C.c, and itzako.c are Object(Matching), while itmaplib.c is explicitly Object(NonMatching).
Moved large bodies of code/data into new item TUs: it_279C.c added 4442 lines, itmaplib.c 1039, itzako.c 723, itmaterial.c 463, itanimlist.c 421, iteffect.c 403, and ithitbox.c 334.
Deleted src/melee/it/it_3F14.c in this resplit context, while adding the new file set and headers.
Added and adjusted headers so external users include the narrower module headers instead of depending on it_2725.h for everything.

Changed fighter and ground users to include the more specific headers where applicable, e.g. ftCo_HammerWait.c and fighter.c include it_279C.h, grzakogenerator.c includes itzako.h, and several ground files include ithitbox.h.

Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to reflect moved/localized data, including converting several it_* globals and jumptable names to local @NNN labels and adjusting sbss object sizes from 0x8 to 0x4 for several it_804D6D28-family symbols.

Renamed some item data/variable types visible in the excerpt, including it_27CF_DatAttrs to itYoshiEggLay_DatAttrs, it_279D_ItemVars to itPokemonSpawn_ItemVars, and it_2F28_DatAttrs to itUnk2_DatAttrs.
Bot report showed the resplit improved linked/matched coverage while causing expected per-unit 'broken matches' from symbols leaving it_2725 and reappearing under the new TUs.
smart_moves: Used repeated floats and strings as clustering evidence for where the original monolithic TU should be cut, rather than splitting only by arbitrary address boundaries.

Kept weak names conservative: the author noted that it_2725 purpose remained unclear and that Codex's suggested itcommon name was not adopted; likewise it_279C kept an address-style name despite a low-confidence itspecial suggestion.

Preferred source-backed filenames where available: itmaplib from __assert("itmaplib.c", ...), itanimlist from __assert("itanimlist.c", ...), itmaterial from the it_MObjInfo it_803F1F90 object context, and itzako inferred from the "can t init zako pos\n" string.
Made the build configuration honest by marking itmaplib.c NonMatching while the other split-out TUs were marked Matching.
Introduced narrow headers for the new modules, reducing future accidental dependence on the catch-all it_2725.h.
Scoped symbols as local where the resplit made file-local compiler-generated constants/jumptables clearer, using @NNN labels instead of misleading global it_* names.
decomp_lessons: For oversized item TUs, repeated literals, asserts containing filenames, named data objects, and diagnostic strings can provide practical split boundaries even when function purposes are not fully known.
A resplit can temporarily look like many broken matches in the old unit; compare with new matches in the destination units before treating the report as a regression.
When a module's semantic name is uncertain, keeping the address-based filename is preferable to committing a speculative name that future work may need to undo.
After splitting a TU, immediately update include sites to module-specific headers so dependencies reveal the actual ownership of functions and types.

Keep NonMatching status at TU granularity when one extracted module is not fully matching; this lets the rest of the split land and improves linked-data/code accounting without overstating completion.

Symbol scope and object-size metadata in symbols.txt may need revisiting after a split because constants that were previously exposed as global placeholders can become local labels tied to the new TU.
naming_conventions: New item module filenames use the existing item prefix: ithitbox, itmaplib, itmaterial, iteffect, itanimlist, itzako.
Address-style names remain acceptable when purpose is uncertain: it_2725 and it_279C were retained rather than renamed to speculative itcommon or itspecial.

More descriptive item-specific struct/type names were introduced where evidence was stronger, e.g. itYoshiEggLay_DatAttrs replacing it_27CF_DatAttrs and itPokemonSpawn_ItemVars replacing it_279D_ItemVars.
A generic-but-less-addressy placeholder name, itUnk2_DatAttrs, replaced it_2F28_DatAttrs in forward declarations.
Local compiler/data labels in symbols.txt use @NNN or local labels such as ...data.0 when the data should not be treated as a global named item symbol.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Resplit GALE01 address ranges in splits.txt across .text, .data, .rodata, .sdata, .sbss, and .sdata2 to preserve section layout from the original it_2725.c block.
Classified each new object separately in configure.py, allowing itmaplib.c to remain NonMatching while other extracted units count as Matching.
Used filename-bearing asserts and strings as high-confidence source-file boundary evidence.
Localized rodata/sdata/sdata2 constants and jumptables to @NNN labels to align with compiler-local data emission after the split.
Adjusted symbol metadata such as sbss sizes and scopes where the prior monolithic split obscured actual object boundaries.
Preserved matching progress by moving code/data wholesale into new TUs rather than attempting semantic rewrites first.
review_feedback: Reviewer ribbanya approved the PR with the comment: "This is really useful, thank you."
No inline review comments were present in the provided PR dump.

Automated decomp.dev report recorded positive aggregate progress: matched code 68.60% (+0.11%, +4396 bytes), linked code 41.36% (+0.90%, +34912 bytes), matched data 41.17% (+1.40%, +17004 bytes), linked data 34.93% (+1.42%, +17212 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching melee/it/itmaplib.c.
Find later PRs that rename it_2725 or clarify its module purpose.
Find later PRs that rename it_279C or split its Pokémon/character/monster/stage-item tables further.
Audit remaining broad includes of it/it_2725.h after this PR for opportunities to use ithitbox.h, it_279C.h, itzako.h, or other narrower headers.
Check whether the type renames itYoshiEggLay_DatAttrs, itPokemonSpawn_ItemVars, and itUnk2_DatAttrs were refined in subsequent item cleanup PRs.
